Exception under § 4 of the Tree Protection Ordinance and § 39 Abs. 5 BNatSchG:
Allowed Actions:
Felling of the construction-impeding maple (Tree Nr. 1) between October 1 and February 28.
Carrying out the requested works in the protected crown and root area of the beech (Tree Nr. 2) under the supervision of a certified tree expert.
Tree Preservation:
Other protected trees must be preserved and protected according to DIN 18920 and environmental regulations throughout the construction process.
Additional Conditions:
Replacement Planting:
The felling permission is expressly conditioned on fulfilling the replacement planting.
Replacement planting must be maintained permanently; in case of loss, equivalent replacements must be planted.
Special Tree Protection:
Compliance with tree protection guidelines and supervision by a certified tree expert must be proven to the designated authority before starting work.
Compensation Payment:
A compensation amount of €5,000 must be paid for nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures.
The compensation for the felled maple is calculated based on the required value calculation model, resulting in a need for 7 replacement trees or €7,000. Two replacement trees are to be planted on-site, and the remaining value is settled with a €5,000 compensation payment.
Additional Information:
Notice: The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions and does not authorize the commencement of work.
Fee: To be determined in a separate notice.

4. 8. An offset payment of €20,000 must be made for financing nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures.

10. The outdoor areas must be created in accordance with § 2 of the development plan LM 20 and § 9 paragraph 1 HBauO until the final completion of the building. In case of failure, equivalent replacements must be planted. (see also points 6 to 10 in the decision)

2.text: Other protected plants must be preserved permanently and protected according to DIN 18920 or nature conservation requirements throughout the construction period.

3.text:
Replacement planting and greening measures must be implemented as per the attached plans (Attachment 9/19):
   - Planting of 5 medium to large-crowned trees (one each of Hornbeam, European Beech, Field Maple, Swedish Whitebeam, and Wych Elm), quality 3 x transplanted, trunk circumference 18-20 cm.

The following building code deviation is permitted according to § 69 HBauO:
   - Deviation from § 33 Abs. 1 HBauO to waive the requirement for a necessary stairwell for the residential building of building class 3 according to § 2 Abs. 3 HBauO.

Reason:
The waiver is granted because the following conditions from the Building Inspection Service (BPD) 05/2012, Fire Protection Technical Interpretations (BTA) page 37, are met:
   - The building is occupied by a single family and stands free.
   - The building has no more than four stories (ground floor, two upper floors, and attic).
   - The basement is fire-separated.
   - The hallway in the stairwell is equipped with a smoke and heat exhaust opening in the ceiling in the attic floor.

Suspensive Condition:
The permit may only be used after the remaining protected trees and hedges on the property and adjacent properties are secured according to the applicable regulations (DIN 18 920 with RAS-LP 4, Hamburg Tree Protection Ordinance, ZTV Tree Care 2017) before demolition and construction begin. The root areas of protected trees must be secured with a fixed fence at least 2 m high, encompassing the crown area plus 1.5 m. If this is not possible, the trunks must be protected with cushioned planks. For hedges, the protection must include the hedge and at least 1 m distance. building_regulations_and_requirements.4_if_irregularities_in_the_subsoil_disco: 040/428 40-2300, must be informed (§ 1 Abs. 1 Hamburgisches Bodenschutzgesetz).
_notes: 1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.

1. On the mentioned property, there is an area no. 7620-101/00 (former gas station) classified as "completed (was not AvF)" registered in the soil condition register of the Authority for Environment and Energy (BUE). During construction measures and earthworks, additional costs for the disposal of excavated soil or construction pit water may arise.

2. The property is located in a project area of the Authority for Environment and Energy (BUE) (GasBW-000), which includes an area of the Elbmarsch, where organic soft layers (clay, mud, and peat) are present in the subsoil. Due to decomposition processes in these soils, soil gases (methane [CH4] and carbon dioxide [CO2]) can naturally form. These gases can rise to near-surface soil layers, accumulate especially under sealed/built-up areas, and potentially penetrate buildings. However, according to current knowledge, these soft layers are not present in relevant thicknesses (> 2m) for the formation of soil gases in the area of the property. If soft layers with a thickness > 2m are encountered or known in the area of the construction project, structural safety measures to prevent gas accumulations and gas entries into the building must be provided, such as a gravel filter layer, gas-tight pipe penetrations, no enclosed spaces below the floor (§ 16 HBauO).

3. Topsoil must be preserved in a usable condition and protected from destruction or waste (§ 202 Baugesetzbuch). Excavated material, including topsoil, must be reused or recycled according to the applicable waste and soil protection regulations of the Federal Republic and the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg in accordance with § 6 Kreislaufwirtschaftsgesetz (KrWG). If reuse or recycling is not feasible according to legal requirements, the excavated material must be disposed of. For the reuse of excavated material suitable for creating a rootable soil layer or for placing in rootable soil layers due to its properties (humus content, absence of contaminants, structure), the requirements of § 12 Bundes-Bodenschutz- und Altlastenverordnung (BBodSchV) must be observed.
5. The start of construction must be reported to the building supervisory authority at least one week in advance (§ 72a Abs. 4 HBauO).
6. The intended use of the building must be reported to the building supervisory authority at least two weeks in advance (§ 77 Abs. 2 HBauO).

1.text: Tree protection requirements for further planning and execution must be strictly followed according to the tree expert opinion (attachment 6 / 16).

2.text:
Before starting construction, the root area of the trees and hedges to be preserved must be protected according to the specifications of a tree expert (e.g., with a fixed tree protection fence). The protection must be carried out by a certified tree care company. If this is not possible, a trunk protection according to DIN 18920, section 4.6, must be installed, and the root area protected with steel plates on a gravel bed with a fleece underlay according to DIN 18920, section 4.12. The tree protection fence installation must be reported to the responsible office.

Tree Protection Site Management:
- All planning and construction processes in the tree environment must be checked, approved, and supervised by a tree expert. This applies to sealing and unsealing work, building construction, and outdoor facilities construction. Tree-friendly planning adjustments must be developed as needed.
- The tree protection site management must be named to the district office in advance. The tree expert must certify the proper implementation of measures monthly and after all work is completed, with the reports sent directly to the mentioned office.

Species Protection:
- Compliance with species protection regulations must be ensured. It is forbidden to catch, injure, kill, or destroy the habitats of protected species according to § 44 BNatSchG. Before starting work, the entire area must be thoroughly inspected for relevant structures, and the absence of prohibited conditions must be documented. For exceptions, the Authority for Environment, Climate, Energy, and Agriculture, N3 Species and Biotope Protection, must be contacted.
